subject: English
chapter: Chapter 2: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

Passages.chapter_name: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
Passages.paragraph: Nelson Mandela was born in the small village of Mvezo in South Africa. From an early age, he was aware of the injustice faced by black people under the apartheid system. Life in his village was simple but harsh, and Nelson observed the struggles of his community. The discrimination he witnessed inspired him to seek equality and justice. Even as a young boy, he dreamed of a South Africa free from racial oppression.
questions: Where was Nelson Mandela born?
What social issues did he observe in his village?
How did these experiences influence his thinking?
Which words show his awareness of injustice?
Why is this paragraph important for understanding Mandela’s early life?

Passages.chapter_name: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
Passages.paragraph: Mandela pursued his education despite numerous obstacles. Attending school gave him discipline and a sense of responsibility. He listened to stories of leaders and heroes and was inspired to fight for what was right. Mandela excelled academically and was determined to make a difference. Education became the foundation for his lifelong commitment to justice and equality.
questions: What role did education play in Mandela’s life?
What challenges did he face in pursuing education?
How did stories of leaders influence him?
Which words show his determination?
How does this paragraph contribute to the theme of perseverance?

Passages.chapter_name: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
Passages.paragraph: As he grew older, Mandela became involved in political activism. He joined organizations that fought against apartheid and worked tirelessly to raise awareness of the injustices faced by black South Africans. Despite threats and imprisonment, he continued to advocate for equality. Mandela’s courage and perseverance inspired many to join the movement. His vision of a free and democratic South Africa became the guiding force in his life.
questions: How did Mandela get involved in activism?
What challenges did he face in his political work?
Which words describe his courage and determination?
How did Mandela inspire others?
What is the main idea of this paragraph?

Passages.chapter_name: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
Passages.paragraph: Mandela’s belief in justice was unwavering. Even when imprisoned for twenty-seven years, he never lost hope. He continued to write and communicate his ideas, inspiring both fellow prisoners and the international community to fight for equality.
questions: What does this paragraph say about Mandela’s belief in justice?
How long was Mandela imprisoned?
What did he do during imprisonment?
Which words show his unwavering spirit?
How did he inspire others while in prison?

Passages.chapter_name: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
Passages.paragraph: Upon his release, Mandela focused on reconciliation and nation-building. He worked tirelessly to unite people across racial divides and promoted democracy. His leadership and vision earned him worldwide respect and recognition.
questions: What did Mandela focus on after his release?
How did he work to unite people?
Which words describe his leadership qualities?
Why was Mandela respected worldwide?
What is the main idea of this paragraph?
